title: YouTube Video Editor — Faceless Finance Channel (Shorts + Long-Form)
description:
Job Type: Part-Time ? Full-Time potential

Rate: $4–$6/hour (based on experience)

Hours: 20–25 hrs/week (weeks 1–2) ? 15–20 hrs/week steady state

Schedule: Flexible — async, deadline-based



About Us

We are launching a new Faceless finance YouTube channel covering topics like investing, personal finance, wealth building, and money strategy. Our audience is serious about their financial future — and our content needs to match that credibility.

We are building this the right way — with the right editor from day one.



What You'll Be Doing

Daily (Shorts):

- Edit 1 YouTube Short per day (57 sec or less) 

- Fast-paced, hook-driven cuts designed to stop the scroll

- Add captions, on-screen text, transitions, and relevant B-roll



3× Per Week (Long-Form):

- Edit 9–12 minute Faceless long-form finance videos

- Sync voiceover or AI talking head footage with contextually accurate B-roll

- Add charts, data overlays, lower thirds, and on-screen callouts where needed

- Maintain consistent brand style, colour grade, and intro/outro templates



How This Role Works — Please Read

-This is a brand new channel. The first two weeks are the most demanding part of this role — not the ongoing norm.

- During weeks 1 and 2 we will be producing and refining a batch of videos together: aligning on style, workflow, feedback loops, and brand consistency. Expect 20–25 hours per week during this period as we build the assembly line together.



Once we are happy with the output and the process is running smoothly, the workload settles into a comfortable, predictable rhythm — 1 Short per day + 3 long-form videos per week at a relaxed, sustainable pace.

We are not a channel that constantly piles on pressure. Once the system works, we protect it.



The Most Important Requirement

- You must have prior experience editing finance videos - This is non-negotiable. 

- Finance content requires you to independently select footage that is contextually accurate — stock charts when discussing market movements, boardrooms for corporate topics, everyday spending scenes for budgeting content. Editors without finance context consistently pick generic or wrong B-roll and it damages channel credibility.



You're a Great Fit If You…

- Have 1+ years editing finance, business, or economics YouTube content

- Are proficient in CapCut, Premiere Pro, or DaVinci Resolve

- Understand YouTube Shorts formatting (vertical, hook in first 2 seconds, captions mandatory)

- Know how to source B-roll that reinforces financial concepts — not just looks nice

- Can turn around a Short same day when needed

- Communicate proactively, flag issues early, and never miss a deadline silently

- Experience launching or working on a new YouTube channel from scratch

- Motion graphics or data visualisation overlays (stock tickers, percentage callouts)

- Familiar with AI voiceover tools (ElevenLabs, etc.)

- Understanding of YouTube retention-optimised editing (pattern interrupts, pacing)



What We Offer

- A structured onboarding period — no pressure to be perfect on day one

- Stable, predictable workload after the initial sprint

- Clear briefs provided every time — no guesswork

- Weekly payment - always on time

- This is a new channel and the rate reflects that honestly. Editors who grow with us will be compensated accordingly when the channel does well — we don't forget who was here from day one.

- Flexible hours — we care about output, not when you clock in



How to Apply - Apply directly through this listing with the following:

- Links to 2–3 YouTube channels or videos you have edited — finance content only; general lifestyle or vlog samples will be disregarded

- A short note (3–5 sentences) on your finance editing experience — what topics, what software, and how you choose B-roll for financial concepts

- Your availability for the first month and your desired hourly rate

- To confirm you have read this entire job post, begin your application with the sentence: "Money never sleeps and neither does good editing." Applications missing this sentence will be automatically disqualified.



?? Only complete applications with finance video samples will be reviewed. We respond to qualifying applicants within 3 business days.

title: Graphic Designer
description:
POSITION SUMMARY

The Graphic Designer will be responsible for facilitating the design process for Cardone Ventures by ensuring that designs are in keeping with the brand's DNA. This includes managing the creative process from start to finish: defining concepts, designing, sketching, and some development.

The Graphic Designer must possess an extensive skill set and qualifications that work together to manage the design process efficiently. This position supports the mission of the organization by demonstrating excellent designs that are compelling to potential customers while also serving the design needs of our internal team to support existing clients.



ABOUT CARDONE VENTURES

Our mission is to help business owners achieve their personal, professional, and goals through the growth of their businesses. We work in dozens of verticals and provide strategic business guidance through courses, live events, partnerships, and investments. Our core values are the backbone of our business and guide our hiring process: we are inspirational, accountable, transparent, disciplined, aligned, and results oriented. This company operates nationally and is growing by the day.



SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

- Consistently producing creative assets, such as graphics for paid media, landing pages, the website and presentations, that not only align with the brand but also drive measurable results. The key is not just making graphics that look good but ensuring they improve engagement, boost conversions, and represent our brand well.

- Consistently incorporating the company's brand values and identity into all creative output. This means ensuring that designs not only meet the team's aesthetic needs, but also deliver a cohesive brand message across all touchpoints.



OBJECTIVES

- Study design briefs and company brand to determine requirements

- Incorporate brand values into content, and direct high-quality creative and brand design

- Develop illustrations, logos and other content using design software

- Leverage knowledge of the latest trends in new media, video, television, design, advertising, and marketing in order to inform creative campaigns

- Develop assets for social media engagement

- Develop assets for websites that will lead to a higher conversion

- Create supporting graphics across various media for A/B testing (social, web, etc.)

- Execute through all design phases, mainly front-end development

- Utilize GIF/sticker content types and creation techniques - animation, stop motion, video, etc. (if necessary)

- Create highly engaging/persuasive graphic designs for all platforms (social media, website,
…etc)



COMPETENCIES

- Proficient in Adobe Creative Cloud Suite and Sketch

- Proficient in major web based "Drag & Drop" web development programs

- Ability to expand and build upon a simple creative brief

- Ability to read creative briefs and convert to functional design

- Background in Keynote, Google Suite, Powerpoint, and Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, After Effects, Illustrator, Premiere, and Animate)

- Understanding of management, transcription, and other administrative procedures

- Demonstrates a focus on creating assets that convert, rather than solely focusing on aesthetics (Functional Design)

- Understanding of conversion statistics and KPIs in order to enhance designs to meet goals

- Understanding of print production and how to submit files to vendors to ensure quality production

- Proficiency in designing for multiple platforms, including paid media, offline assets, websites,
 campaigns, etc.. The designs should be visually appealing, persuasive, and adaptable to different formats.

- Basic knowledge of ClickFunnels software



EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE

- At least 5 years of relevant industry experience (branded content/new media/creative production)

- Digital or Graphic Design degree preferred





PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS

- Prolonged periods sitting at a desk and working on a computer





COMMITMENT TO DIVERSITY



As an equal opportunity employer committed to meeting the needs of a multigenerational and multicultural workforce Cardone Ventures recognizes that a diverse staff, reflective of our community, is an integral and welcome part of a successful and ethical business. We hire local talent at all levels regardless of race, color, religion, age, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation or disability, and actively foster inclusion in all forms both within our company and across interactions with clients, candidates and partners.



If this position caught your eye, send us your resume! For best consideration, please complete the online application form:


/

title: Creative Website Designer (Website Revamp)
description:
Our Marketing Agency is looking for 1 Creative Website Designer to revamp our Pricing and Portfolio pages. This is a project-based role with long-term potential.



What You’ll Do:



Update our Pricing page to reflect current packages, add new tiers or add-ons if needed, ensure all inclusions are clearly listed, check pricing consistency across the site, and improve the layout to look clean, modern, premium, and easy to skim.



Redesign and expand our Portfolio page to look high-end, improve the Featured Portfolio Categories section, organize projects by category (restaurants, salons, brands, ads, etc.), add more client examples, include short result-based captions (growth, views, wins), and create a smooth, visual-focused scrolling experience.



What We’re Looking For:



Strong modern website portfolio with examples of pricing and portfolio page redesigns.



Excellent layout, branding sense, and attention to detail.



Ability to suggest creative improvements and elevate the brand experience.



To Apply:



Start your application with “REVAMP.”



Attach your previous work portfolio — applications without a portfolio will not be considered.



Include live links to your portfolio (preferred) and 2–3 redesign samples.



Share your rate (hourly or project-based).



We want someone who can elevate our brand and grow with us long-term.

title: Healthcare Call Center / Virtual Assistant - Healthcare experience is required.
description:
We’re a fast-growing ophthalmology practice in California for a skilled and responsible Virtual Assistant to join our call center team! If you’re organized, a great communicator, and love helping people, this is a great opportunity to be part of a supportive and growing team.



In this role, you’ll answer patient calls, schedule and confirm appointments, make outbound calls for reminders and follow-ups.  You'll also help with miscellaneous administrative tasks as needed. You’ll play an important role in keeping our clinics running smoothly and ensuring a great patient experience.  



We’re looking for someone with strong English and/or Spanish communication skills, prior call center or virtual assistant experience, and excellent attention to detail. You should be reliable, able to multitask efficiently, and comfortable handling a high volume of calls.  



The schedule is Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM (Los Angeles time). 



To apply, please send a short introduction about yourself, resume, your relevant experience, a 1–2 minute voice recording, typing speed, and your internet speed test results.

title: Bookkeeping/Quickbooks/Xero
description:
**Job Title:** Bookkeeper Needed (QuickBooks) – Clean Up & Ongoing Support



**Description:**



We are a small, family-owned marketing agency looking for an experienced bookkeeper to help us get our financials cleaned up and organized for tax filing.



We need help reconciling and categorizing transactions for **2024 and 2025**, and preparing clean Profit & Loss statements that we can send to our CPA.



This is initially a **project-based role**, with the opportunity for ongoing monthly bookkeeping if it's a good fit.



---



**Responsibilities:**



* Clean up and organize bookkeeping for 2024 & 2025

* Categorize all income and expenses correctly

* Reconcile bank and credit card accounts

* Prepare accurate Profit & Loss statements

* Ensure everything is CPA-ready

* Communicate clearly on missing items or discrepancies



---



**Requirements:**



* Strong experience with **QuickBooks (Online preferred)**

* Proven experience cleaning up messy or incomplete books

* Understanding of U.S. small business accounting

* Ability to work independently and ask the right questions

* High attention to detail



---



**Bonus (Not Required):**



* Experience working with marketing agencies or service businesses

* Familiarity with Stripe, PayPal, or similar platforms



---



**To Apply:**

Please include:



1. A short summary of your bookkeeping experience

2. Examples of similar cleanup projects you've completed

3. What bookkeeping software you prefer and why

4. Your estimated timeline to complete 2 years of cleanup

5. Your hourly rate or fixed price for this project



Start your application with the word **“CLEANBOOKS”** so I know you read this.



---



**Important:**

We are looking for someone reliable and detail-oriented. Accuracy matters more than speed.



If this goes well, we are open to a long-term monthly bookkeeping relationship.

title: Photo Editing QA and Customer Support
description:
ABOUT REAL TOURS



Real Tours is a fast-growing real estate media company delivering professional photography editing, virtual staging, 3D tours, and listing media to real estate agents and brokers. We pride ourselves on fast turnarounds, exceptional quality, and client relationships that keep agents coming back listing after listing.



ROLE OVERVIEW



We are looking for a versatile QA & Client Support Specialist who can operate across both quality assurance and client-facing support functions. In this hybrid role, you will review edited media for quality before delivery while also serving as a point of contact for client inquiries, re-edit requests, and order updates. This position is ideal for someone who is both a sharp visual reviewer and a confident communicator — someone who understands what great real estate media looks like and can represent that standard to clients.



QUALITY ASSURANCE RESPONSIBILITIES



Review edited real estate photo batches for exposure consistency, color accuracy, sky replacement quality, horizon alignment, and overall presentation

Do virtual staging deliverables for realistic staging, proper scale, material texture, and lighting match to the actual room

Do item removal edits for seamless surface reconstruction with no visible patching or artifacts

Inspecta and create Matterport, Zillow 3D Home, and CubiCasa floor plan and 3D tour 

Revise rejected work for clients and their needs

Track and document recurring quality issues to support editor coaching and process improvement



CUSTOMER SUPPORT RESPONSIBILITIES



Serve as a primary point of contact for real estate agent clients via
, Slack, and internal platforms

Communicate order statuses, delivery timelines, and proactively notify clients of any delays

Process re-edit requests and relay precise client feedback to the editing team

Manage client expectations around same-day and next-day delivery SLAs

Escalate unresolved issues or pattern complaints to the appropriate team lead

Support new client onboarding and guide agents through the revision and delivery process



REQUIREMENTS



1+ years of hands-on experience in real estate photo editing, virtual staging, or media post-production — you must be able to identify AND fix common editing issues, not just flag them

Proficient in Adobe Photoshop — able to perform exposure corrections, sky replacements, color grading adjustments, and clean item removals independently when needed

Advanced virtual staging knowledge: understanding of furniture scale, perspective matching, lighting consistency, shadow realism, and material textures

Advanced item removal skills: ability to reconstruct floors, walls, ceilings, and surfaces convincingly with no visible patching, cloning artifacts, or texture mismatches

Familiarity with real estate photo editing standards: sky replacements, virtual staging, item removals, twilight edits, and exposure corrections

Strong written English communication skills — able to write clearly to both clients and editors

Highly self-managed and organized with the ability to juggle QA reviews and open client tickets simultaneously

Experience with Slack, 
, Notion, or similar remote work tools

Comfortable with fast-paced, high-volume workloads and same-day turnaround expectations



NICE TO HAVE



Direct experience with Aryeo, Matterport, CubiCasa, or Zillow 3D Home

Prior work with a real estate photography/media company

Experience with AI editing tools and awareness of AI artifact detection

Background in virtual staging review or advanced retouching QA



TOOLS YOU WILL USE



Adobe Photoshop / Lightroom

Aryeo

Matterport

Zillow 3D Home

CubiCasa

AI Editing Tools

Slack


 / Notion



Work Schedule:

Tuesday - Saturday | 8am - 5pm EST



HOW TO APPLY

To apply, please submit your resume and a brief cover note describing your relevant experience.

Include links to any relevant work samples, portfolios, or past projects where applicable.

title: Social Media Manager & Video Editor (Full-Time, Long-Term Role)
description:
We are looking for a dedicated Social Media Manager & Video Editor to join our growing team. This is not a side gig or part-time role — this is a long-term, full-time position where you will play a key role in helping our business generate leads and grow.



You will be responsible for editing, publishing, optimizing, and managing content across social platforms with the goal of driving consistent inbound leads.



This role goes far beyond just posting videos. You will be expected to think critically, improve performance over time, and actively contribute ideas that help increase results.



You will also be expected to learn and adapt quickly. We will be using platforms and tools that we may not always be fully familiar with ourselves, which means you must be self-driven, resourceful, and capable of teaching yourself new systems. A strong desire to continuously expand your skills is essential in this role.



You will be treated as a core tea
mber, not just a contractor. We are building something long-term, and we want someone who wants to grow with us — professionally, financially, and personally.

title: Cold Caller (Outbound Sales) - Part-Time
description:
Cold Caller (Outbound Sales)

Overview: We are looking for an energetic Cold Calling Specialist to join our real estate

acquisitions team. In this role, you will make a high volume of outbound calls to homeowners

and gauge their interest in selling their property. Your daily target will be ~75 calls (and up to

250–300 calls on days we use a multi-line dialer). You’ll follow a proven script to identify

motivated sellers, handle initial objections, and transfer hot leads to our acquisitions team. This

is a key role that requires excellent spoken English and a friendly, persistent attitude.

Responsibilities:

? High-Volume Outbound Calling: Make at least 75 calls per day to targeted property

owners using our click-to-dial system. (On certain campaigns with a multi-line dialer, you

may dial 250–300 numbers a day.) Maintain a positive, professional demeanor

throughout all calls.

? Lead Qualification: Follow our prepared script to introduce our company and ask key

questions about the homeowner’s interest in selling. Listen actively to identify motivation,

timeline, and property details.

? Overcome Objections: Professionally handle common pushbacks (“Not interested,”

“How did you get my number?” etc.) with polite and confident responses. Stay resilient

and upbeat even after repeated rejections – it often takes dozens of “no’s” to get a “yes.”

? Record Keeping: Update call outcomes in our CRM in real time. Mark leads as

interested, not interested, wrong number, etc., and add any relevant notes from the

conversation. Accuracy in tagging call results is crucial for follow-up.

? Target Achievement: Strive to meet or exceed weekly and monthly goals, such as

number of leads (interested sellers) generated, appointments set, or transfers to the

sales team. Work with the team to continuously improve performance (e.g. refining the

script or call approach based on feedback).

Requirements:

? Excellent English & Neutral Accent: Fluent spoken English with a neutral or

minimal accent. You should be easily understood by American homeowners. (We will

request a voice recording or conduct a phone screening to verify this.)

? Cold Calling Experience: At least 1 year of experience in outbound calling/telesales,

telemarketing, or a related role. Experience calling US-based customers or real estate

cold calling is a big plus.

? Resilience & Energy: Positive attitude and ability to handle rejection. You can make

hundreds of calls and still keep an enthusiastic tone. Self-motivated and driven to reach

targets.

? Tech Requirements: Comfortable using auto-dialer software and CRM systems.

(Experience with tools like Mojo Dialer or CallTools, and Podio or a similar CRM, is

helpful.) Must have a reliable high-speed internet connection, a quality headset, and a

quiet workspace for making calls.

? Schedule: Availability to work U.S. business hours 12pm-4pm or 3pm-7pm EST. This may be overnight if you live in a different timezone – please ensure you can

commit to these hours long-term.

What We Offer:

? Competitive Base Pay + Bonus: Base salary of $400/month, with performance

bonuses for each deal closed from the leads you generate (we love to reward results!).

? Training & Support: Ongoing training in effective cold calling techniques, real estate

knowledge, and scripts. You’ll have team support and coaching to help you succeed.

? Growth Opportunities: This is a long-term position. Top performers can take on greater

responsibilities over time or grow into other roles (e.g. Lead Manager or Team Lead).

? Team Culture: Join a friendly, results-driven team. We celebrate wins and learn from

losses together. Your contributions will be valued as an important part of our acquisitions

process.

? Remote Work: Work from home and save commute time. We just ask for reliable

attendance and communication during work hours.

How to Apply: To be considered, please send your resume and a voice recording (1-2

minutes) introducing yourself. In the recording, briefly tell us about your cold calling experience

or read a few lines from a script to showcase your accent and energy. You may use a free

service like Vocaroo and share the link. Applications without a voice sample will not be

reviewed, since speaking ability is crucial for this role. We will invite promising candidates for a

live interview and mock call. Excited to hear from you – let’s hit those dialing goals together!

title: AI Tool Developer
description:
Company: FSW Group (Welsford and ValveMan)

Hours: Project-based gig with potential for extended engagement based on value delivered

Reports to: Executive Assistant to the CEO

Compensation: $10 - $15 per hour (negotiated per engagement)



ABOUT US



FSW Group (F.S. Welsford Co.) is a third-generation, family-owned valve and instrumentation distributor headquartered in Exton, PA. Operating under brands including ValveMan and AVCO (Alloy Valves & Controls), we serve OEM manufacturers, end users, and industrial accounts across the mid-Atlantic and Eastern United States. Our team includes inside sales, outside territory managers, and warehouse operations staff. We are growing, investing in technology, and building internal capabilities that give us a durable competitive edge.



ROLE OVERVIEW



We are seeking a skilled, AI-native developer to build and deploy a suite of internal productivity and sales-enablement tools for the FSW Group team. This is a hands-on, execution-oriented role. You will work directly with our Executive Assistant to the CEO to scope, build, document, and deliver working tools that our team can operate and maintain.



This position is project-based to start. Contractors who deliver exceptional results, communicate clearly, and build tools our team actually uses will be considered for longer-term, ongoing engagement. We are not looking for consultants who write specs—we need builders who ship.



WHAT YOU'LL BUILD



Valve Spec Mapping Tool

A web-based or internal app where a user enters simple application

requirements (media, pressure, temperature, size, end connection,

actuation) and the tool returns matched valve products from our existing

vendor catalog, along with the correct vendor point of contact to reach out to

for quote or availability. Must be maintainable and updateable as our vendor roster changes.



Credit Report Analyzer

A tool that accepts an uploaded Experian Business Credit Report (PDF) and

a requested credit amount, then produces clean, structured guidance on

whether to extend credit, at what terms, and key risk flags to consider.

Output should be readable by non-finance staff.



Company Website Rebuild

Design and deploy a clean, professional website for the FSW Group /

Welsford family of companies. Simple architecture, mobile-responsive, easy

for internal staff to update. Full deployment required—not a mockup.



 Scraping / Archive Tool

A tool that connects to our company
 domain and pulls down inbound

and outbound
s for archiving, search, or reporting purposes. Must

handle authentication securely and be deployable within our existing tech

environment.



Additional Tools (TBD)

Additional tools will be scoped based on business need as the engagement

progresses. Examples may include vendor scorecard dashboards, customer

lookup integrations, quote generation aids, and internal knowledge base

tools.



DOCUMENTATION REQUIREMENTS (REQUIRED FOR EVERY TOOL)



Every tool delivered must include the following documentation, written for a non-developer audience:



- A simple, plain-language SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) explaining how the tool works and how to use it

-A troubleshooting guide covering the most likely errors, what causes them, and how to resolve them—including how to prompt an AI assistant (like Claude or ChatGPT) to diagnose and fix issues

- Redeployment instructions: step-by-step guidance on how to redeploy or restart the tool if it goes down, is moved, or needs to be updated

- Dependency and environment documentation: what the tool runs on, what accounts or credentials it needs, and where those are stored



Documentation must be delivered alongside the working tool—not as an afterthought.



IP & OWNERSHIP REQUIREMENTS



This is non-negotiable: all work product produced under this engagement—including all code, scripts, configurations, documentation, and derivative works—is the sole intellectual property of F.S. Welsford Co. / FSW Group. You will be required to sign a work-for-hire / IP assignment agreement prior to beginning any

project.



- No subscriptions may be used without prior approval

- No proprietary frameworks that create lock-in to a vendor without our explicit consent

- All credentials,



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR:



Required:

Proven track record of building and deploying working tools using AI assistance (Claude, GPT-4, Cursor, etc.), not just prototypes

Ability to build full-stack or no-code/low-code tools that non-developers can operate

Experience writing clean documentation and SOPs for non-technical users

Strong communication skills; able to translate vague business requirements into working software

Comfortable working independently with minimal hand-holding

Portfolio of AI-assisted tools you have shipped and maintained (required for consideration)

Developing tools that can “self-fix”



Preferred:

Experience in B2B, industrial, distribution, or manufacturing environments

Familiarity with
 systems, ERPs, or CRM integrations

Experience with web scraping, PDF parsing, or document analysis tools

Knowledge of web deployment (VPS, Netlify, Vercel, or equivalent)

Background in writing work-for-hire agreements or IP transfer documentation



HOW TO APPLY



Fill out the Application form below:

title: Video Editor – Long-Form Cinematic Adventure
description:
We are looking for a highly skilled YouTube video editor to transform raw footage into immersive, cinematic experiences. This role isn't about fast-paced "retention editing"; it’s about building an atmosphere, mastering sound design, and letting the environment tell the story.



This channel is dedicated to sharing outdoor adventures through a lens of tranquility and awe. We believe that the most powerful stories aren't told through frantic cuts or loud personalities, but through the landscape itself. Our mission is to transport the viewer to the world’s most remote and beautiful destinations, providing a digital sanctuary that emphasizes the scale, silence, and serenity of the natural world.

We are looking for a dedicated Video Editor who can transform raw field footage into polished, immersive long-form experiences. This is a role for a storyteller who enjoys the "slow cinema" process—someone who can find the narrative thread in hours of wilderness footage and weave it into a cohesive journey.



Your day-to-day will involve:

 • Narrative Curation: Reviewing 2–3 hours of raw footage to select the most impactful clips that build a 20–60 minute story.

 • Color Grading: Bringing LOG footage to life with a "true-to-life" aesthetic—natural, vibrant, and sharp.

 • Atmospheric Audio: Crafting a seamless audio flow where the environment speaks as loudly as the visuals.

 • Musical Curation: Selecting tracks from our licensed library that complement, rather than overpower, the scene.

We operate under a "Place over Personality" philosophy. Your editing style should be cinematic and invisible—the viewer should not feel it’s over edited.

 • Pacing: Slow and immersive. We want shots to breathe, giving the viewer time to register the emotion of a location.

 • Subject Integration: We use people primarily for scale and perspective. We favor motion transitions (e.g., a subject walking through the frame) to move between scenes naturally.

 • The Invisible Observer: The camera is a witness, never an intrusion. Your edits should maintain this sense of being a quiet observer of the journey.

TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

 • Software Proficiency: Expert-level command of DaVinci Resolve or Final Cut Pro.

 • Workflow: Experience managing a 4K 10-bit workflow and large file transfers.

 • Color Mastery: Proven ability to grade LOG footage to a professional, natural finish.

 • Sound Design: You must be skilled at working primarily with natural ambient sounds recorded in the field. While we provide a music library, the "music" of the wind, footsteps, and water is the priority.

 • Attention to Detail: A high standard for technical polish, from frame-accurate cuts to clean audio transitions.

 • Infrastructure: A reliable, high-speed internet connection for consistent file synchronization.

HOW TO APPLY To ensure we are a good creative match, please follow these steps carefully:

 1 Review the Benchmark: Watch this video 
 to understand our required look, feel, and pacing. If you can meet or exceed this quality, we want to see your work.

 2 Portfolio: Provide a link to your portfolio, specifically highlighting cinematic or outdoor adventure work. Applications without a portfolio link will not be reviewed.

 3 Software Experience: Tell us about your journey with DaVinci Resolve or Final Cut Pro. How many years have you used them? What are your strengths, and what areas are you currently developing?

 4 Technical Specs: Please include your current internet upload/download speeds.

 5 Submission: Send your application to our hiring
. Note: Your subject line must include the word “mabusisi” to be considered.

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to complete a paid test edit to demonstrate their grasp of our specific pacing and color requirements. We look forward to seeing your vision.

title: Premium Social Media Manager (Luxury Home Builder | Lead Generation Focus)
description:
Cogan Custom Homes

Marsden Cove, Whang?rei, New Zealand



We are a well-established, high-end custom home builder with over 30 years’ experience, specialising in architecturally designed waterfront and canal homes across Northland.



Our work is premium. Our clients are discerning. And our marketing needs to reflect that.



We are looking for a skilled, proactive Social Media Manager to take ownership of our online presence and help generate genuine, high-quality enquiries.



This is a long-term, part-time role for someone who can think strategically, execute independently, and take pride in building a premium brand.



Hours & Pay



• 15–20 hours per week

• Flexible (overlap with NZ business hours preferred)

• $10–$15 NZD/hour (based on experience and results)



Your Role



• Manage and grow our Instagram and
 accounts

• Plan, create, and schedule high-quality content using our professional photos and videos

• Write captions that position us as the premium waterfront home experts

• Promote our new waterfront show home and key projects to generate enquiries

• Support and improve our Google presence (local SEO, keywords, Google Business profile, reviews)

• Help improve our visibility for high-end home building searches in Northland

• Track performance weekly and suggest improvements that drive real leads—not just likes



How We Work



We operate on an 80/20 ownership model:



• We provide strategy, direction, brand assets, and approvals

• You take ownership of execution, ideas, and day-to-day management



We are not looking for someone who just follows instructions.

We want someone who thinks, improves, and takes initiative.



What We’re Looking For



• Proven experience managing and growing social media accounts (links required)

• Strong copy writing and content creation skills

• Excellent eye for premium branding and visual storytelling

• Ability to work independently and meet deadlines

• Working knowledge of SEO and Google tools (especially for local businesses)

• Reliable, organised, and proactive communicator



Bonus (Not Required)



• Experience in real estate, construction, architecture, or luxury brands

• Video editing / Reels experience

• Event or launch promotion experience



IMPORTANT – How to Apply



To be considered, your application must include:



Links to at least 2 social media accounts you have managed (include results if possible)

A short explanation of how you would grow a high-end home builder’s Instagram

One specific example of when you took initiative and improved results

Briefly explain how you would improve our visibility on Google for:

“custom waterfront homes Northland”

Start your application with the word: Waterfront

Loom Video (Max 5 minutes – camera on)



Please record a short Loom video answering:



Your experience managing social media accounts (especially premium/lifestyle/real estate/construction)

How you would adapt our brand voice to attract serious, high-end enquiries

A time you took initiative and improved results

The tools you use for content, scheduling, and analytics—and why

Shortlisting & Next Step



We will shortlist the top 3 candidates and invite you to complete a paid test task ($75–$100 NZD).



You will be asked to promote our new waterfront show home as if you were already part of the team.



We are looking for initiative, creativity, and premium positioning that generates real interest and enquiries.



This is a long-term opportunity for someone who wants to grow with a high-end brand and take pride in their work.



If that sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you.

title: JUNIOR/MID-LEVEL WORDPRESS & JAVASCRIPT DEVELOPER
description:
JUNIOR/MID-LEVEL WORDPRESS & JAVASCRIPT DEVELOPER

Studio SWEAT onDemand | Remote | Contractor | Part-Time with Growth Path | $25/hr + bonus



---



ABOUT US



Studio SWEAT onDemand (SSoD) is a subscription-based online fitness platform built for people who want real workouts on their schedule: spin, strength, HIIT, yoga, and more. We serve a growing community of members through our web platform and native apps, and we partner with corporate wellness programs and gyms around the country.



We're a small, focused team running a real product at real scale. No enterprise bureaucracy, no bloated processes. Just a tight-knit group solving practical problems and building something people genuinely use every day.



---



THE ROLE



We're looking for a dependable, execution-focused developer to take on a meaningful share of our day-to-day platform work. Our infrastructure runs on a heavily customized WordPress stack, and there's a significant amount of maintenance, debugging, and incremental improvement work that needs consistent, capable hands.



This role exists so our Lead Developer can focus more on architecture, strategy, and our upcoming platform evolution. You'll work closely with them, getting clear direction, real context, and quick feedback.



We're planning a transition toward a modern JavaScript-driven architecture over the next 12-24 months. If you're strong in WordPress today but excited about growing into modern JS work, this is a role where that progression happens naturally.



One non-negotiable: we are an AI-native team. We expect you to already be using tools like Cursor, Claude Code, or GitHub Copilot as a core part of how you work, not as an experiment you're considering. If that's not already your workflow, this probably isn't the right fit.



This is not a senior architect position. It's the right fit for a strong junior-to-mid level developer who does solid work, communicates clearly, and wants room to grow.



---



WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON



In the near term:

- Debugging and fixing issues in production: WordPress site, custom plugins, and REST API integrations

- Maintaining and improving custom PHP plugins and our WordPress theme

- Implementing small features and enhancements based on internal priorities

- QA and regression testing after changes

- Working with third-party APIs and service integrations

- Maintaining technical documentation as the codebase evolves

- Supporting internal tooling and developer utilities

- Backlog work that hasn't had bandwidth: unit tests, code cleanup, performance audits, and refactoring fragile areas ahead of the rebuild



As the role grows:

- Contributing to feature development in a modern JavaScript stack

- Supporting the platform rebuild and migration away from legacy WordPress components

- API development work in support of native apps

- Building new features and integrations

- Taking on increasing ownership of development areas over time

- Interfacing with other tea
mbers and external vendors as needed



Ongoing:

- Close collaboration with the Lead Developer

- Leveraging AI tools to work efficiently and ship quality work

- Self-organizing around a task list with clear direction but minimal hand-holding

- Proactive communication on blockers and progress



---



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



Required:

- Solid WordPress development experience: themes, plugins, hooks, custom functionality

- PHP: you can read, write, and debug it confidently

- [removed] modern JS, not just jQuery basics

- MySQL: comfortable writing and optimizing queries

- REST API experience: consuming and working with external APIs

- Git and GitHub: you use version control properly and without being prompted

- AI-native workflow: you already use tools like Cursor, Claude Code, or GitHub Copilot as a core part of how you work. This is a firm requirement, not a nice to have.



Nice to have:

- WooCommerce and WooCommerce Subscriptions experience

- Modern JS frameworks (React, Next.js, Vue, or similar)

- CI/CD familiarity (GitHub Actions or equivalent)

- Docker or Linux server environments

- Headless CMS or decoupled frontend experience



You're probably a good fit if you:

- Prefer solving real problems over building toy projects

- Communicate proactively: you ask questions early instead of going quiet

- Can self-organize around a task list without daily hand-holding

- Take quality seriously without over-engineering simple things

- Get genuinely excited about working in a codebase that has history and wants to evolve



---



WORK ENVIRONMENT



- Part-time to start, with a clear path to grow as the role and platform expand

- Contractor role

- Reasonable overlap with US business hours expected

- Direct working relationship with the Lead Developer

- Small, no-nonsense team: you'll know what's going on and your work will be visible



---



COMPENSATION



$25/hr + bonus, with compensation reviewed based on experience and performance



---



ROOM TO GROW



This role starts part-time, but that's a starting point, not a ceiling. We're a small team with real technical ambition, and we invest in people who show up and do good work. The platform rebuild we're planning is a significant project, and the right person will have the opportunity to grow alongside it in hours, responsibility, and ownership.



We're not looking for a contractor to plug a gap. We're looking for someone who could become a core part of the team over time.



---



HOW TO APPLY



To be considered for this role, please submit a short video (2-3 minutes) introducing yourself. In your video, cover:



- A brief overview of your background and experience

- The types of WordPress projects you've worked on

- A WordPress bug you debugged that turned out to be harder than expected and how you worked through it

- Your experience with WooCommerce or subscription billing systems, if any

- What AI development tools you currently use and how they fit into your day-to-day workflow

- Why this role makes sense for where you're at right now

 

Applications without a video will not be reviewed.

 

In addition to your video, please separately include:

 

- A link to your GitHub profile or examples of relevant work (portfolio, live projects, private repo descriptions, etc.)

- A brief note on your current availability and any other ongoing commitments we should know about



We move quickly with candidates who are a strong fit. We'll ask for a small paid technical exercise before any final decisions: straightforward, practical, and respectful of your time.

title: Part-Time Video Editor & Graphic Designer for DTC Social Content (Paid + Organic) — Long-Term Role
description:
Job Description:

I'm looking for a skilled video editor and graphic designer to join me long-term as part of a small, fast-moving creative team. You'll be producing short-form video ads and organic social content — a mix of talking-head edits and motion graphics videos — plus supporting static graphics. 



Briefs will be provided for each piece of content, but if your style fits well, there's real potential to take creative control over time.



This is a part-time role (20 hours/week) to start, with strong potential to grow into full-time with a salary increase based on output quality and reliability. I want someone I can work with for the long haul.



Detailed Responsibilities:

Edit short-form video content (typically 15–60 seconds) for paid social and organic channels

Produce talking-head edits with captions, b-roll, sound design, and pacing that holds attention

Create motion graphics videos (kinetic text, animated product/feature highlights, transitions)

Design static graphics and carousels for organic social

Work from written briefs with supplied raw footage, reference material, and brand assets

Polish, retouch, and Photoshop AI-generated content (your own outputs and work produced by other tea
mbers) to a finished, on-brand standard

Deliver finished content same day on the tasks assigned

Manage your tasks and deliverables in ClickUp

Communicate via ClickUp (primary) and WhatsApp when needed — not daily check-ins, just as needed



Required Technical Skills:

Adobe Premiere Pro (primary video editing tool)

Adobe After Effects or similar motion graphics tool

Adobe Photoshop

Strong graphic design fundamentals — typography, layout, hierarchy, color

Ability to match an established brand style from references



Desired Technical Skills:

Canva and CapCut

Experience with AI creative tools, especially Higgsfield AI or any AI that it provides, is a big bonus. We use Higgsfield and other AI tools as part of our creative pipeline, and you'll be expected to polish AI-generated content to a finished, on-brand standard.

An eye for paid-social performance (what stops the scroll)



Soft Skills:

Reliable same-day turnaround

Takes direction well but brings ideas

Clear written communication in English

Organized in ClickUp



What Matters More Than Years of Experience:

A strong portfolio is far more important than years on a CV. If your reel is great, apply — even if you're early in your career.



How to Apply:

To show me you've read the post, start your application with the word CITRUS. Applications that don't will be ignored.



Please include:

Your portfolio / reel (links only)

2–3 examples of short-form video ads or organic social videos you've edited

1–2 examples of motion graphics work

If you've used AI tools (Higgsfield, Runway, Kling, Midjourney, etc.): outline which tools you've used, your workflow, and include 1–2 portfolio examples of AI-generated content that you polished / finished in Photoshop or your editing software

Your estimated turnaround time for each of the following, assuming brief + raw assets are provided:



A 30-second talking-head video (cuts, captions, b-roll, sound)

A 30-second motion graphics video

Your Premiere Pro and Photoshop experience in 2–3 sentences

title: Full Time Remote Paralegal and Client Support for Estate Planning Law Firm
description:
We are a U.S.-based law firm focused on estate planning, probate, and asset protection.

 

We are hiring a full-time remote paralegal based in the Philippines who can handle both case work and client communication, including outbound calls.

 

This is a long-term role for someone who is highly organized, detail-oriented, and has impeccable English communication skills.

You must be comfortable following structured processes exactly. This is not a role for improvisation.

Because in estate planning:

creativity = errors

consistency = quality

 

What You’ll Be Doing



This role combines legal support, operations, and client communication.

Estate Planning Case Support

Review client-provided information (CIMI forms, Asset Inventories, questionnaires)

Identify missing, inconsistent, or unclear information

Prepare and format estate planning documents using firm templates (Wills, Trusts, POAs, etc.)

Ensure documents are complete, internally consistent, and aligned with client instructions

Assist with document revisions and updates

Support attorneys in preparing files for document review meetings and signing ceremonies

 

Operations & Case Management



Maintain detailed case tracking systems (Excel / Clio Manage/ Lawmatics/ SharePoint)

Track client progress through the estate planning process

Ensure all required information and documents are collected before drafting

Keep internal systems accurate and up to date

Help improve workflows and internal processes

 

Client Communication (Outbound Calls Required)



Make outbound calls to clients to follow up on missing information, documents, and next steps

Communicate clearly, professionally, and confidently with U.S.-based clients

Explain next steps in the estate planning process (training provided)

Handle sensitive conversations with professionalism and patience

Document all communications accurately

 

This Job Is For You if:



You are an attorney

have near-perfect English (written and spoken)

You sound clear, professional, and confident on phone calls

You are extremely detail-oriented and organized

You can identify inconsistencies in information and ask the right questions

You can switch between deep focus work and client interaction

You take ownership of your work and follow through

You can work U.S. business hours (8:30 am to 5:30 pm Eastern Standard Time)



Required Skills

 

Excellent spoken English (neutral accent, easy to understand)

Strong written English (grammar and clarity matter)

Experience with outbound calls or client communication

Strong Excel, Word, and Office 365 skills

Ability to manage multiple deadlines and priorities

Experience working with structured client data or document preparation



 

Technical Requirements



Reliable computer capable of handling document-heavy work.

      RAM: 16 GB

      Processor: Intel i5/i7 (10th gen+) or Ryzen 5/7

      Storage: SSD (512 GB preferred)

High-speed and stable internet connection

      Download: 50+ Mbps

      Upload: 20+ Mbps

Quiet, professional work environment suitable for client calls

Headphones or audio equipment for clear communication

Ability to use secure access tools (VPN)

Ability to work without interruptions during U.S. business hours

 

“Please confirm in your application that you meet all technical requirements.”

Candidates who do not meet these technical requirements will not be considered.

 

Bonus Skills (Preferred but Not Required)



Experience working with U.S. law firms

Estate planning or probate experience

Familiarity with CRMs or case management systems (Clio Manage, Clio Draft, Lawmatics, Zoom, Teams, etc.)

 

Why This Role Is Different

 

You will be involved in both legal work and client interaction

Your work directly impacts families planning for their future and protecting their assets

We value precision, organization, and clear communication

Opportunity for long-term growth within the firm

 

Compensation

 

Competitive monthly salary (based on experience)

Performance-based increases

Stable, long-term position

American Holidays off

10 Vacation Days per year

5 Sick days per year

 

What We Will Need From You to Apply:

 

A short introduction

Speed test screenshot

Computer RAM and Processor screenshot 

A description of your experience with outbound calls

Your experience with Excel or tracking systems

Any legal or document-related experience

A video recording (3 minutes) introducing yourself and explaining your experience and why you are the best fit for this role

A writing sample (Do not use AI tools for this response. We are evaluating your natural writing ability.)

 

 

Prompt 1 for Writing Sample:

A client provides you with an incomplete Asset Inventory and a partially filled estate planning questionnaire.

 

Explain how you would:

Review the information

Identify missing or inconsistent details

Organize the information

Prepare it so that an attorney can efficiently draft estate planning documents

 

Prompt 2 for Writing Sample:

A client has not responded to requests for important information needed to complete their estate plan.

 

Write a message 
 or call script) explaining:

Why the information is important

What is needed from them

Encouraging them to respond promptly in a professional and respectful way

title: Hero Wanted – Student Housing Operations & Leasing Coordinator
description:
TL;DR – Role Summary



Founder-led student housing company looking for a detail-obsessed, judgment-driven property manager. This is a long-term role built on trust, ownership, and clear communication.



You are going to be the HERO in what we do here — we offer reliable, modern student living for college students in the U.S. We have properties in the states of California, New York, and Arizona.



WHY work with us



Our mission is to provide reliable, high-quality modern housing for engineering students who are working incredibly hard to build their futures. We take pride in being the kind of landlord students can count on.



Our core values: Humility. Integrity. Ownership. Excellence.



We are a small and nimble team. You will be one of the first people we bring on — think of it as joining a founding team early. You will get to learn, grow, and help shape how things are done as we scale.



Here's what's in it for you:

- Work fully from home — no commute, no gas, no office politics

- Freedom and independence to manage your work and schedule

- Build a diverse skill set across leasing, customer service, marketing, sales, and more

- Work with people who are humble, considerate, and genuinely open to feedback

- Be part of something meaningful — helping hard-working students find a safe, reliable place to live



If you have a great idea, pitch it. We grow faster through shared thinking and honest feedback.



This role starts at 10–20 hours per week during training. If things go well — and we expect they will — you'll likely move to 30–40 hours per week soon after. We're looking for someone who wants to grow with us long-term, including into other businesses we plan to expand into.



Note: This is an individual contributor role to start. "Coordinator" refers to managing properties, processes, and responsibilities — not managing people, yet.



HOURS



Training / Probation: First 1–2 months, Monday–Friday, 9:30am–12pm EST, approximately 20 hours per week (actual hours subject to small variations).



Official work hours: 35–40 hours approximately; hours are for reference and will adjust slightly based on actual needs.



Regular Off-Peak Season: July–February (8 months)

- Monday–Friday: 9am–4:30pm EST (37.5 hrs)

- Saturday OR Sunday: 2.5 hrs of your choice.





Peak Leasing Season: March–June (4 months), 2 weeks in late August (Move In).

- Monday–Wednesday + Friday: 12–8pm EST (32 hrs) 

- Saturday OR Sunday: 10am–6pm EST (8 hrs; you pick the day)



Hours subject to slight variation as business needs evolve.



During all official hours: available to answer calls immediately and respond to text messages within 30 minutes.



Weekend availability in EST is a firm requirement. Students are frequently home on weekends and leasing tours are commonly scheduled on weekends. This is non-negotiable.



The nature of property management fluctuates throughout the year. Peak periods include leasing season (March–June) and move-in/move-out season (May–August). Slower months may include additional admin or business support tasks.



THE REALITY OF THE ROLE



We want to be upfront about what this job actually looks and feels like day-to-day.



The hours listed above are your availability window — not a guarantee that you'll be working intensively every minute of it. The role is genuinely flexible. You don't need to sit in front of your computer constantly. During quieter periods, you can be laid back. But when you're needed, you're really needed — and that means immediate responsiveness, no exceptions.



Workload throughout the year: The workload naturally peaks and dips with the academic calendar. During leasing season (March–June), expect a full workload of roughly 40 hours per week with open houses, lead follow-ups, and lease coordination happening simultaneously. During the off-peak season (July–February), the pace slows significantly and the workload drops to roughly 10–20 hours per week focused on maintenance and routine tasks.



Here's something we want to be upfront about: during the quieter months, you likely won't be working a full 40 hours every week — and you'll still get paid for it. That's intentional. We need you available and ready for when things come up, and when a real emergency hits, we need you fully present, no questions asked. That happens very few times a year. The rest of the time? Pretty chill. We think that's a fair trade, and we want the right person to see it that way too.



Emergency situations: When a real emergency happens — a gas leak, water leak, or urgent tenant situation — we need you to drop everything and be fully present until it's resolved. No delays, no "I'll get to it later." These 
nts are rare. When they do hit, you may need to work intensively for a day or two straight. We recognize that and will give you flexibility — extra time off or a lighter workload — in the days after to make up for it.



The bottom line: this is not a rigid desk job, but it is a high-responsibility one. We trust you to manage your time well, stay on top of your tasks, and show up fully when it matters most.



SALARY

$5–$7/hr. We believe in happy, fairly compensated employees.



Please share:

- How much you would like to earn per hour to start

- Where do you see yourself professionally in 1 year?

- Where do you see yourself professionally in 5 years?



Please be realistic — the higher your ask, the higher our performance expectations will be.



Responsibilities



You will help manage the entire life cycle of a student tenant: Leasing ? Move-in ? Maintenance ? Move-out.



Leasing

- Post rental ads

- Qualify and respond to leads

- Coordinate open houses

- Host open houses on Google Meet

- SELL the rentals to the leads, convince them to sign — through VIDEO/PHONE CALLS

- Assist with lease signing and deposit coordination



Move-In

- Coordinate move-in logistics

- Provide keys or access codes

- Ensure move-in instructions are completed



Maintenance

- Intake maintenance requests from tenants

- Escalate and approve maintenance requests per guidelines

- Contact vendors for repairs

- Follow through to confirm repairs are completed

- Help mediate and resolve tenant-to-tenant conflicts with professionalism and neutrality, escalating to Landlord when needed



Move-Out

- Coordinate move-out process

- Ensure tenants follow move-out instructions

- Review and assess move-out photos

- As trust builds, this role may expand to include other responsibilities such as general admin, business analytics, and business support — inbox organization, research, scheduling, and document preparation. These tasks will always be clearly scoped and secondary to real estate operations.



Required Skills & Experience

Prior real estate or property management experience is required. We do not have the bandwidth to train someone starting from zero — we need someone who already understands the basics and can hit the ground running.



Beyond experience, we are looking for someone who:

- Remembers what we discussed and follows through without reminders

- Understands WHY we do things a certain way, not just what to do

- Thinks independently and proactively — identifies issues before they become problems

- Can problem solve, make decisions, and execute on ideas with minimal hand-holding

- Is open to being trained to eventually think like us



When you can make good decisions independently and we can trust you to handle our properties without us watching — that's when promotions and more responsibility happen. 



Communication & Tools

- Strong English proficiency, written and verbal

- Comfortable using tools such as:

- Property management software (MagicDoor, AppFolio)

- Google Suite (Google Forms, Sheets, Doc, Gmail, etc.)

- Text-based communication systems (Quo/OpenPhone)



Non-Negotiables (Please Read Carefully)



Extreme attention to detail — no typos on critical information (phone numbers,
s, tenant names, addresses, etc.). The expectation is that you double-check your work before submitting.



First critical error: warning and retraining

Second critical error: we may need to reassess whether this is a good fit

Honesty and reliability matter much more to us than speed.



If you think we could be a good fit, please APPLY with us by filling out this Google Form below:







Next Steps:

1. Application review — about 1–2 weeks from submission

2. Interviews — selected applicants will be invited to a brief interview

3. Trial Tasks — finalists from interviews will be invited to participate in a series of Trial Tasks

title: AI Video Specialist (AI generative Content - Full-time)
description:
About Hustl

Hustl is a holding group behind a portfolio of consumer brands spanning home decor, wellness, and smart home security. We're an operator-led team of 60+ across 10 countries, doing over $20M+ in annual revenue with more than $30M deployed into paid social since inception. Our brands have shipped to 42+ countries and been seen over a billion times on social feeds. We're not an agency. We're operators building the next generation of consumer brands.



The Role

We're hiring a full-time AI Video Specialist to create high-performing video ads across our entire portfolio. One day you're producing a lifestyle ad for our home decor brand, the next a wellness reel, the next a security-camera demo. Every concept lives and dies on the feed, and you own the entire creative pipeline from script to final cut.

No prior AI video experience required. If you have taste, hustle, and the drive to learn, we'll train you on every tool in the stack (Kling, Runway, Sora, Seedream, Nano Banana Pro, Premiere, After Effects, CapCut).



What You'll Do

Write scripts and storyboards that hook in the first two seconds. Generate, edit, and refine AI-powered video ads for Meta, TikTok, and YouTube. Produce scroll-stopping short-form content across all of our brands. Iterate on winners using real ad performance data. Ship volume. Own visual consistency across each brand's identity.



What You Bring

A sharp eye for what makes an ad actually perform. A hunger to learn. Solid video editing fundamentals (Premiere, After Effects, CapCut, or DaVinci). Written communication strong enough to pitch a concept in three sentences. Strong internet connection and a quiet workspace. Bias for action.



Nice to Have

UGC-style content experience. Photography or videography background. Familiarity with Meta Ads Library and ad performance metrics. A portfolio you're proud of, AI-generated or otherwise.

Compensation & Perks



?100,
,000 PHP monthly base salary. Performance bonus for every winning ad you ship, uncapped. 9am - 6pm Philippines Time, no graveyard shifts. Remote-first. Full AI tool stack covered. Friday team hangouts with games, giveaways, and espresso machines as prizes.



Why This Role Matters

Our ads have been seen over 1.4 billion times. Every one was built by a small creative team that believes in speed, taste, and obsessive iteration. Your work ships to millions, gets measured in real data, and shapes how our brands grow.



At Hustl we plant a tree for every order placed across our portfolio. We're on track to hit 1 million trees planted by the end of 2026. Join us, make ads that matter, and help us get there.

title: Customer Support (Written) | No Experience Needed | FUN| Dance Academy
description:
***READ FULL JOB POST BEFORE APPLYING. *** Please don't waste your time or ours. Thanks :)



Full-time, long-term opportunity.



Four days per week, or five days per week scheduling options.



Looking for:

- Outstanding English

- Empathy



Our communications with students are FRIENDLY, FUN, and PERSONAL.



ABOUT US

We are the largest salsa & bachata dance academy in Los Angeles and are growing rapidly.



The culture here is FUN, fast, and friendly. Our goal is to make every student feel welcome and completely taken care of.



Dancing changes lives - we are on a mission to deliver the best salsa/bachata educational experience on Earth.



The company is MaS Dance Academy.





ABOUT YOUR TEAM

Working with a remote team that helps with all facets of the business, you'll form a close working relationship and bond with your team mates. We even host team retreats in the Philippines!



----------------------------

SCHEDULE

All times written in PST (Los Angeles time)



TRAINING (2-4 weeks):

Sunday - Thursday 9am - 6pm (with 1 hour break)



AFTER TRAINING



Both options are full-time 40 hours per week.



OPTION 1 - FIVE DAY SCHEDULE:

Thursday - Monday 8am - 5pm (with 1 hour break)



OPTION 2 - FOUR DAY SCHEDULE:

Saturday, Tuesday, Wednesday 10am - 9pm (with 1 hour break)

Friday 12pm (noon) - 11pm (with 1 hour break)

----------------------------



YOUR ROLE

• Your primary role is written student support (text,
 etc.)

• When there are no support tasks to complete, you'll contribute in a wide variety of other ways, depending on your skills and interests. Some examples may include writing blogs, updating websites, doing research, collecting and analyzing numerical data and using it to create reports, etc.





WRITTEN VS PHONE SUPPORT

• 95% of our student support is written. However, occasionally you will need to be on the phone with a student. You must be comfortable on the phone.





TYPES OF SUPPORT YOU'LL HANDLE

• One on one texting and
ing

• Mass texting and
ing

• Occasional phone calls (rare)

• Updating database after every interaction and attempted interaction

• Daily reports

• Etc.





COMPANY CULTURE

Working in a small business like ours is very different from a large corporation. Some of the differences are:



• Personal tone: Our communications with students are FRIENDLY, FUN, and PERSONAL. It's like talking to your best friend. We use emoji's and exclamation points and are very personal. We take the opposite approach of large corporations that give robotic, frustrating, not very helpful support. You need to be intuitive and have strong emotional intelligence to feel what the students are needing when they communicate.



• More creativity: there is more freedom to adjust your responses for each scenario. This is more challenging than just following strict templates, but also more stimulating and exciting.



• Impact: your actions will have a big impact on the business, which you will clearly see



• Feedback: your feedback on what we are doing well, and what could be better, is not only valued, but is mandatory! We want everyone here thinking as if they were the business owner.





ABOUT YOU

• Perfect written English

• Available full-time

• High emotional intelligence

• Flexible

• Quick learner

• Thrives in fast-paced, quickly changing environment

• Desire to always seek better ways to do things, including finding ways to use A.I. tools

• Happy to be on the phone when needed (which is rarely)

• Tremendous attention to detail - gets things right the first time

• PRO-ACTIVE. Always looking for ways to improve.

• Meticulous, organized, methodical. Almost never misses a detail.

• Critical, creative thinker. Not robotic. Always looking for better ways to do things. Always considering all the angles of a task.

• Not afraid to share your opinion - we NEED to hear your opinion on the approaches that we take so that we can improve.

• Able to ask clarifying questions in order to get tasks right the first time

• Strong communicator

• Makes decisions independently

• Quick learner

• Fast computer

• Fast, dependable Internet

• Bonus if you have experience or interest in salsa or bachata or any dance!





APPLY NOW. FORMAT YOUR APPLICATION AS A NUMBERED LIST WITH THE FOLLOWING:

1. A brief intro about yourself



2. Are you happy to be on the phone when needed?



3. This is a fast-paced environment with quickly changing conditions. You’ll need to be able to think quickly on your feet and track dozens of moving pieces at once with precision and accuracy. This role is NOT for people who feel overly anxious in this type of environment. Apply only if you thrive in such an environment. Based on this information, do you still feel this role is a good fit for you?



4. Complete this form: 
/
. Write in the subject line of your application the answers to the assessment in the link above: 1. Grammar score 2. Typing WPM 3. Numbers assessment answer (regarding achieving $2,000 profit) 4. Highest level of formal education



6. Any customer support experience? If yes, please describe.



7. Which of the two scheduling options do you prefer?



8. These positions are competitive, often with hundreds of applicants. What is your competitive requested monthly payment for this full-time position?





(Don't
 us off the platform or message on social media - your application will be disqualified. Don't worry, we personally check every application here)



Looking forward to meeting you!

title: Social Media Specialist
description:
Content Creation: They design, create, and schedule posts (images, videos, and text) for various social media platforms (e.g., Instagram,
,
, LinkedIn). This involves crafting engaging and creative content that aligns with the brand's message and goals.



Strategy Development: They develop and implement social media strategies aimed at increasing brand awareness, engagement, and follower growth. This can involve planning campaigns, setting goals, and analyzing trends.



Community Engagement: They interact with followers by responding to comments, messages, and mentions. They might also monitor social media conversations relevant to the brand and engage with potential customers or clients.



Analytics & Reporting: Using tools like Google Analytics, social media insights, and other analytics platforms, they track the performance of social media campaigns. They use this data to optimize future content and campaigns, identifying what's working and what isn't.



Brand Monitoring: They keep track of brand mentions and industry trends, ensuring the brand maintains a positive image and responding to any potential negative comments or issues.



Paid Social Media Advertising: Often, they create and manage paid ad campaigns (e.g.,
 Ads, Instagram Ads) to reach a broader audience, boost engagement, and achieve marketing objectives.



Collaboration: They often work with other departments (e.g., marketing, design, and PR) to align social media efforts with overall brand initiatives, promotions, or product launches.

title: SOC Engineer (Level 2)
description:
Please apply for this job here: 



JOB SUMMARY

We are looking for an experienced SOC Engineer - Level 2 to join our Security Operations Center (SOC) team. As a Level 2 SOC Engineer, you will play a critical role in monitoring, analyzing, and responding to security i
. You will be responsible for handling more complex security threats, guiding Level 1 engineers, and contributing to the overall security posture of the organization. This role requires strong technical expertise, excellent analytical skills, and the ability to work in a fast-paced environment



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

• Advanced Threat Detection and Response: Monitor and analyze security alerts and i
, conduct in-depth investigations, and respond to complex security threats, ensuring they are mitigated effectively.

• I
 Management: Take the lead in managing security i
, coordinating with other IT and security teams to contain, eradicate, and recover from security breaches.

• Forensic Analysis: Perform detailed forensic analysis on compromised systems to identify root causes, attack vectors, and indicators of compromise (IOCs).

• Vulnerability Management: Assist in the identification, assessment, and remediation of vulnerabilities within the organization’s infrastructure.

• Security Tool Management: Maintain and optimize security tools and technologies, including SIEM, IDS/IPS, firewalls, and endpoint protection systems.

• Collaboration: Work closely with Level 1 SOC Engineers, providing guidance, mentorship, and training to help them improve their skills and effectiveness in responding to security i
.

• Documentation: Create and maintain detailed documentation of security i
, forensic analysis, and response actions, ensuring compliance with organizational policies and industry standards.

• Reporting: Generate and analyze security reports, providing insights and recommendations to improve the organization’s security posture.

• Continuous Improvement: Stay updated on the latest cybersecurity threats, vulnerabilities, and best practices, and contribute to the ongoing improvement of SOC processes and technologies.

• On-Call Support: Participate in a rotating on-call schedule to provide after-hours support for critical security i
.



TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

Proven expertise in the following areas is required: 

- Strong knowledge of security monitoring tools (e.g., SIEM, IDS/IPS) and their configuration and management.

- Proficiency in network security, endpoint security, and i
 response techniques.

- Experience with forensic tools and methodologies for analyzing compromised systems.

- Familiarity with scripting and automation (e.g., Python, PowerShell) to streamline security processes.

- Knowledge of cloud security practices and tools (e.g., AWS, Azure) is a plus.



QUALIFICATIONS

• Education: Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity, Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent experience.

• Experience: 3-5 years of experience in a SOC or cybersecurity role, with at least 1-2 years at a Level 2 position or equivalent.

• Certifications: Relevant certifications such as CompTIA Security+, CompTIA CySA+, or equivalent are preferred.



Preferred Qualifications

MSP background or experience managing security for multiple client environments.

Experience with vulnerability management and patch orchestration tools

LOCATION, SCHEDULE & COMPENSATION



This is a full-time remote role for candidates residing in the Philippines.  The schedule is Monday through Friday, 6am-3pm EST.  The salary is $2500/monthly.

title: Real Estate Research Analyst
description:

Your Mission

This role ensures our team pursues only qualified opportunities by providing accurate title intelligence, ownership insights, and financial risk analysis.



Position Summary

We are seeking a highly detail-oriented Real Estate Research Analyst with strong research ability and preferably title examining or title underwriting exposure. Title companies

Title examiners

Escrow teams

Underwriters

Real estate attorneys

Probate research

Preferred Experience

Title examiner experience

Worked with title company or underwriter

Real estate legal research

Curative title exposure

Real estate transaction coordination

Technical Skills



Salesforce preferred

Personal Traits

High integrity

Process driven

Coachability

Analytical mindset





We are looking for investigative thinkers who enjoy solving ownership puzzles, not just performing data entry.



We value accuracy over speed.
